description: Real-world example: Plugin hook for automatic Python code formatting with Black

content.name: python-formatter-plugin
content.description: Automatically formats Python files with Black after writing/editing
content.author: Your Name

content.description: Automatic Python formatting with Black. Runs after any Write or Edit operation on Python files.

hook_script.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Plugin Hook: Python Code Formatter
# Automatically formats Python files with Black after writing/editing
# License: MIT

set -e  # Exit on error

# Color codes for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'  # No Color

# Get plugin and project directories
PLUGIN_HOME="${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}"
PROJECT_HOME="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}"

# Setup logging
LOG_DIR="${PROJECT_HOME}/.claude/logs"
mkdir -p "${LOG_DIR}"
LOG_FILE="${LOG_DIR}/format-python.log"

# Function: Log message
log_message() {
  echo "[$(date +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')] $1" >> "${LOG_FILE}"
}

# Function: Check if file is Python
is_python_file() {
  local file="$1"
  [[ "$file" == *.py || "$file" == *.pyi ]]
}

# Function: Check if Black is installed
check_black_installed() {
  if ! command -v black &> /dev/null; then
    echo "${YELLOW}⚠️  Black formatter not installed${NC}" >&2
    echo "   Install with: pip install black" >&2
    log_message "Black not installed, skipping format"
    return 1
  fi
  return 0
fi

# Main execution
main() {
  # Read hook input from stdin
  local input
  input=$(cat)

  # Extract tool information
  local tool_name
  tool_name=$(echo "$input" | grep -o '"tool_name":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4 || true)

  local file_path
  file_path=$(echo "$input" | grep -o '"file_path":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4 || true)

  # Validate we have required information
  if [[ -z "$file_path" ]]; then
    log_message "No file_path in input, skipping"
    exit 0
  fi

  # Skip non-Python files
  if ! is_python_file "$file_path"; then
    log_message "Not a Python file: $file_path"
    exit 0
  fi

  # Skip if file doesn't exist
  if [[ ! -f "$file_path" ]]; then
    log_message "File not found: $file_path"
    exit 0
  fi

  # Check Black availability
  if ! check_black_installed; then
    exit 0  # Don't fail, just skip
  fi

  # Attempt formatting
  local filename
  filename=$(basename "$file_path")

  log_message "Formatting: $file_path"

  if black "$file_path" --quiet 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "${GREEN}✓ Formatted${NC}: $filename" >&2
    log_message "Successfully formatted: $file_path"
    exit 0
  else
    echo "${YELLOW}⚠️  Format check failed${NC}: $filename" >&2
    log_message "Format check failed for: $file_path"
    exit 0  # Non-blocking error
  fi
}

# Execute main function
main

behavior.trigger: After any Write or Edit operation on .py or .pyi files
behavior.action: Automatically runs 'black' formatter on the file
behavior.error_handling: Non-blocking - if Black fails, doesn't interrupt workflow

environment_variables_used.CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT: Path to plugin directory for script location
environment_variables_used.CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR: Path to project root for logging

testing: Create a Python file with unformatted code in Claude Code
File should be formatted with Black

customization.add_black_options: Modify black command with flags like --line-length, --target-version
customization.support_other_formatters: Copy script pattern for other Python formatters (autopep8, yapf)

examples: Linter plugin: Validates code style BEFORE formatting (use PreToolUse)
Type checker plugin: Checks types AFTER formatting (use PostToolUse with different tool)
Documentation plugin: Generates docstrings (use on Write operations)
multi_plugin_composition.execution_model: All plugins' hooks execute in parallel, no guaranteed order
multi_plugin_composition.design_consideration: This plugin is self-contained and doesn't depend on other hooks

advanced_features.description: Load .claude/.python-formatter-config to customize Black options per project
advanced_features.implementation: Add to script: read config file and pass options to black

advanced_features.description: Only format files matching certain patterns (src/, tests/, etc.)
advanced_features.implementation: Add path checking in hook script before formatting

advanced_features.description: Exclude files with .no-format suffix or in .formatignore
advanced_features.implementation: Check file name/path before executing Black

security_considerations: Logging goes to project-local logs (not system-wide)
Black is a trusted, well-known formatter
benefits: Works with Claude's file editing workflow
limitations: Only triggers on Write/Edit (not on Bash operations)
Adds slight latency after each file edit
Multiple plugins may format multiple times (acceptable overhead)
